skill.name: game-test-case-generator
skill.description: Generate structured game QA test cases from requirement documents, text notes, CSV files, and prototype screenshots. The skill supports full test case documents, quick review checklists, and optional Markdown-to-Excel or Xmind conversion.
skill.summary: Turns game feature requirements into structured QA test cases and review checklists.
skill.author: snake-mustang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk alerts, but review found they are mostly false positives from Chinese Markdown text, fenced examples, repository URLs, and template content. No prompt injection attempt, hidden exfiltration path, runtime network call, dynamic code execution, or weak cryptographic implementation was found. The only confirmed concern is low-risk local filesystem access in optional converter scripts that read user-provided Markdown and write user-selected output files.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Write in Optional Converter Scripts
low_findings.description: The converter scripts read a user-provided Markdown file and write Excel or Xmind Markdown output. This is an intended local conversion feature, but users should choose output paths carefully because existing files can be overwritten.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly open local input and output files, and the paths come from command-line arguments. The behavior is clearly documented as a conversion feature and does not show exfiltration or hidden file access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown fences and usage examples in documentation and skill instructions. They are inert text unless a user deliberately runs the documented commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences or examples, not executable code paths. No subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec call was found in the scripts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs Are Not Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are a license badge, repository clone URL, and GitHub profile links in README.md. The reviewed Python scripts do not make HTTP requests or open sockets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL evidence is limited to documentation links. A targeted review found no requests, urllib, socket, fetch, or similar network API usage in the code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto and high-entropy alerts map to Chinese text, Markdown tables, example files, and parsing code. No cryptographic API, encoded payload, packed binary, or decryption routine was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious locations contain normal text or Markdown parsing logic. The scripts import only argparse, pathlib, typing, re, pandas, and openpyxl, with no crypto or obfuscation libraries.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms in Test Content
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts correspond to the Chinese word for system or to compatibility test examples. They describe game QA scenarios and do not inspect host configuration or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are user-facing compatibility test cases for iOS, Android, and device display behavior. There is no code that enumerates host operating system details or hardware.

content.user_title: Generate Game QA Test Cases from Requirements
content.value_statement: Game QA teams spend hours turning feature requirements into complete test coverage. This skill converts requirements into structured test cases, quick checklists, and export-ready documentation.
actual_capabilities: Creates full game test case documents with IDs, priorities, preconditions, steps, and expected results.
Creates quick test point checklists for review sessions and early requirement validation.
Organizes coverage across normal, boundary, exception, performance, security, compatibility, and UI scenarios.
Marks unclear, missing, or conflicting requirements as confirmation items with recommended handling.
Provides optional local scripts to convert generated Markdown into Excel or Xmind-importable Markdown.
limitations: It depends on the quality and completeness of the provided requirements.
Prototype image analysis depends on the vision capability of the host AI tool.
Generated cases still need review by QA leads and product owners.
The converter scripts handle the documented Markdown formats and may need changes for custom templates.

use_cases.title: Prepare Feature Test Plans
use_cases.description: Convert a new game feature specification into detailed manual QA cases before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review Requirements Quickly
use_cases.description: Generate concise test points and unclear requirement questions for sprint planning or product review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Documentation
use_cases.description: Produce consistent Markdown, Excel, or Xmind-ready outputs for teams with shared QA templates.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Test Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate full game test cases for this login feature requirement. Include normal, boundary, and exception scenarios, plus any unclear requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Quick Review Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Fast review before a planning meeting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quick mode to create a test point checklist for this game event requirement. Group points by core, boundary, exception, performance, security, compatibility, and UI coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Requirements into Exportable Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this CSV requirement file and generate a full Markdown test case set. Use stable case IDs, priorities, steps, expected results, and a requirements mapping table.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Coverage and Ambiguity
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced QA review of an existing draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these generated game test cases against the original requirement. Find coverage gaps, duplicated cases, vague expected results, and missing confirmation items.

output_examples.input: A requirement describes account login with password length rules, lockout after five failures, and a two-second response target.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a login test suite with normal login, password boundary checks, lockout errors, network failure handling, response time checks, and security validation points.

output_examples.input: A product manager provides a limited-time roulette event specification with rewards, probabilities, entry points, and unclear edge cases.
output_examples.output: The skill creates grouped test points for reward deduction, prize delivery, probability display, UI states, anti-cheat checks, device compatibility, and unresolved requirement questions.

output_examples.input: A QA lead needs an execution-ready document for a new shop feature.
output_examples.output: The skill generates Markdown cases with case IDs, priority levels, preconditions, step-by-step actions, expected results, exception branches, and summary statistics.

best_practices: Provide acceptance criteria, UI notes, limits, and error states with the requirement material.
Ask for full mode when cases will be executed by testers, and quick mode for early review.
Review confirmation items before locking the final test plan.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated cases as final without QA and product review.
Do not omit numeric rules, platform targets, reward limits, or timing requirements.
Do not run converter scripts on unreviewed paths that may overwrite important files.

faq.question: What inputs can this skill use?
faq.answer: It can use Excel, CSV, text requirements, and prototype images when the host AI tool supports image understanding.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: It generates Markdown by default and includes optional scripts for Excel and Xmind-importable Markdown conversion.

faq.question: Can it generate quick test points instead of full cases?
faq.answer: Yes. Quick mode produces concise review checklists grouped by test scenario type.

faq.question: Does it replace QA review?
faq.answer: No. It accelerates drafting, but QA leads and product owners should verify coverage and expected results.

faq.question: Does the skill send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of runtime network access was found in the included scripts. Any AI model processing depends on the host tool.

faq.question: Are the conversion scripts required?
faq.answer: No. They are optional local utilities for teams that need Excel or Xmind-compatible outputs.
